title: Plant Material Preparation and Salt Imposition for PlantScreen Analysis
author: Mariam Awlia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.4xzgxp6
materials: Put agar into a 200 mL beaker­­.
Put in microwave to dissolve for 00:03:00 .
Cover beaker with foil or cling film.
FC measurement with Light curve protocol.

steps.title: Add 200 mL of normal water
steps.instruction: Add 200 mL of normal water.

steps.title: Add 100 µL to each mini tube
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL to each mini tube.

steps.title: Pot sieved soil for germination trays...
steps.instruction: Pot sieved soil for germination trays (G Trays with 24 pots).

steps.title: Remove excess water from all trays...
steps.instruction: Remove excess water from all trays and leave to drip for one day before sowing.

steps.title: Prepare traylist for Plantscreen and ID stickers for pots
steps.instruction: Prepare traylist for Plantscreen and ID stickers for pots.

steps.title: Sow the germination trays according to the Master GTray List
steps.instruction: Sow the germination trays according to the Master GTray List. ¬30 seeds per pot per accession.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 L of water to saturate the soil and cover with transparent lid.

steps.title: Cut the end of a pipette tip
steps.instruction: Cut the end of a pipette tip. Use a pipette and the wide tip to sow seeds. Seperate seed by pipetting in a line.

steps.title: Put trays in the growth chamber with conditions set at
steps.instruction: Put trays in the growth chamber with conditions set at: %s %s

steps.title: Remove lids after 3-5 days, earlier...
steps.instruction: Remove lids after 3-5 days, earlier if tray lids are too humid.

steps.title: Pot sieved soil for Plantscreen trays...
steps.instruction: Pot sieved soil for Plantscreen trays (P Trays with 20 pots), while weighing each pot to have ± 60 g of soil. Tare one empty pot and then weigh each pot + soil.

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 L of water to saturate the soil and cover with transparent lid.

steps.title: Remove excess water from all trays...
steps.instruction: Remove excess water from all trays and leave to drip for one day before transplanting.

steps.title: Let plants grow for 1 week, then perform transplantation
steps.instruction: Let plants grow for 1 week, then perform transplantation. Choose similar sized seedlings.

steps.title: Transplant 2-3 seedlings per pot by...
steps.instruction: Transplant 2-3 seedlings per pot by taking seedling with soil around it.

steps.title: Cover with transparent lids for 3-5 days
steps.instruction: Cover with transparent lids for 3-5 days. Do not cover completely. Leave an open area to reduce humidity formation.

steps.title: Put trays in the growth chamber with conditions set at
steps.instruction: Put trays in the growth chamber with conditions set at: %s %s Arrange trays to be in batches of 6 on the shelves for maximum light incidence on plants (no shadow).

steps.title: Remove extra seedlings (thin-out) after 1 week
steps.instruction: Remove extra seedlings (thin-out) after 1 week.

steps.title: Register trays in PlantScreen by using...
steps.instruction: Register trays in PlantScreen by using the import function in the PlantScreen Registration program.

steps.title: Add the reference weight of all...
steps.instruction: Add the reference weight of all the trays using the Plant Watering program.

steps.title: Water trays in PlantScreen to the...
steps.instruction: Water trays in PlantScreen to the specified percentage of field capacity.

steps.title: Let plants grow until they reach...
steps.instruction: Let plants grow until they reach the 10-leaf rosette stage (approx. 21-23 days old) to apply salt stress.

steps.title: Water trays in PlantScreen to the...
steps.instruction: Water trays in PlantScreen to the specified percentage of field capacity the day before salt imposition.

steps.title: Prepare weigh boats of NaCl in...
steps.instruction: Prepare weigh boats of NaCl in 2L bottles for salt imposition using distilled water (dH 2 O).

steps.title: Prepare bottles of dH 2 O for control plants
steps.instruction: Prepare bottles of dH 2 O for control plants.

steps.title: Remove all Australian pots to ease...
steps.instruction: Remove all Australian pots to ease the process of salt imposition the next day.

steps.title: 250 millimolar (mM) NaCl ( 29
steps.instruction: 250 millimolar (mM) NaCl ( 29.22 g ) in 2 L of water for 01:00:00 bottom-watering using black trays and small pots only.

steps.title: RGB, FC (Lightcurve) and IR, takes...
steps.instruction: RGB, FC (Lightcurve) and IR, takes about 1 hour and 20 mins. 01:20:00 Make sure to stay committed to this timing for future scannings, because time of day can have a profound effect.

steps.title: Turn off all lights
steps.instruction: Turn off all lights.

steps.title: Turn IR light on 100%, others off
steps.instruction: Turn IR light on 100%, others off.

steps.title: Turn on the 3 lights at 22%
steps.instruction: Turn on the 3 lights at 22%.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1% agar to eppendorfs, vortex well.

steps.title: Check the eppendorf tubes the next day
steps.instruction: Check the eppendorf tubes the next day. Vortex again to ensure the seeds are dispersed in solution.

steps.title: Place in fridge at 4 °C for 72:00:00
steps.instruction: Place in fridge at 4 °C for 72:00:00 .
